data.name: Company has an approved Third-Party Management Policy
data.description: This test verifies that your company has an approved [Third-Party Management Policy](/policies/third-party-management-policy-bsi) in Vanta.
data.failureDescription: You don't have an approved Third-Party Management Policy yet.
data.remediationDescription: Use our [policy templates](/policies?policy=Third-Party%20Management%20Policy) to create and approve a custom Third-Party Management Policy for your company.

data.name: Company completes security assessments for relevant vendors
data.description: This test verifies whether vendors requiring security assessments have current and up-to-date assessments according to their risk levels.
data.failureDescription: Some vendors do not have an up-to-date security assessment.
data.remediationDescription: Visit the [Vendors page](/vendors) and complete security assessments. To complete an assessment, open the assessments tab on each vendor. Upload the latest security documentation, document any notable findings, and mark the assessment as complete.

data.name: Vendors list maintained
data.description: This test verifies that you have manually added at least one vendor (other than automatically integrated accounts) on the [Vendors page](/vendors) that is visible to auditors. If you do not have any vendors beyond what Vanta integrates with, you can deactivate this test.
data.failureDescription: You haven't manually added any vendors on the Vendors page.
data.remediationDescription:
On the [Vendors page](/vendors), ensure you've added all vendors that your company uses. Vanta will auto-populate any vendor we integrate with, but you should manually add any other vendors to ensure they're tracked in one place.

If you do not have any vendors beyond what Vanta integrates with, you can deactivate this test.

data.name: Vendors assigned risk levels
data.description: This test verifies that every vendor listed on your [Vendors page](/vendors) has been assigned a risk level. Assigning risk levels to vendors is a foundational part of vendor risk management, enabling organizations to prioritize security reviews and due diligence based on the level of risk each vendor poses.
data.failureDescription: Some of your vendors don't have a risk level specified on the Vendors page.
data.remediationDescription: Go to the [Vendors page](/vendors) and assign a risk level for each vendor.
